channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18813] memory FS based OSD with write-back support
item.description:
Implement an memory-based OSD device with writeback support for Lustre.

It borrows lots of design from memory-based file systems such as tmpfs/ramfs.

The data is frist written into memory-based file system (called MemFS in short).

And then, the data can be flushed to the persistent storage in a delayed write-back manner.

 

TODO

  • limiting for inodes and blocks.
  • lprocfs support. Track OSD stats and access them via lprocfs.
  • Use Maple Tree in new kernel to manage and access entries within a directory and Indexing access for FID seq and persistent node map setting, etc, al.
  • Implement the functionalirty needed by LFSCK.
  • Using a single storage target for a combined OST/MDT/MGT.
  • Quota support.
  • Writeback support with ldiskfs/ZFS or KV store as persistent backends.
  • Add transcation support.
item.summary: memory FS based OSD with write-back support

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58637
Subject: LU-18813 osd-wbcfs: store and pin OST objects in MemFS
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 639c83a489b627270ff359146c226e9a96cb17e6

Hi Tim,

Thanks for your review and testing.

There are many shared codes between MetaWBC, OSD-wbcfs and tmpfs/ramfs.

However, there are some places different.

For example, MetaWBC use Lustre super block/inode/dentry for MemFS, OSD-wbcfs has its own suerblock/inode/dentry and we have extend the @dir_context to get FID of a file during readdir().

All these need to be abstracted.

Thanks for uploading the prototype Yingjin - I'll give it a look. I also have some OSD tests in https://github.com/koverstreet/ktest/tree/master/tests/fs/lustre - so I'll give those a try and let you know how it goes. About https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57522/5, I wonder if the memFS code should live in it's own directory (like lustre/wbc/ or something) if this code might be shared across Lustre?

Hi, Tim,

I just uploaded the whole runnable patch above for your reference.

It implemented bulk I/O with hole support and dirent in MemFS.

The mount and indexes codes are borrowed from your mem-OSD work.

I did some simple tests such file creation/delete, read/write, it works.

[root@qcn tests]# FSTYPE=wbcfs REFORMAT=yes ./llmount.sh 
mgs: CentOS Linux release 8.4.2105
MGS_OS_ID_LIKE=rhel fedora centos
MGS_OS_VERSION_ID=8
MGS_OS_ID=centos
MGS_OS_VERSION_CODE=134217728
mds1: CentOS Linux release 8.4.2105
MDS1_OS_VERSION_ID=8
MDS1_OS_VERSION_CODE=134217728
MDS1_OS_ID_LIKE=rhel fedora centos
MDS1_OS_ID=centos
ost1: CentOS Linux release 8.4.2105

OST1_OS_VERSION_ID=8

Checking servers environments
Checking clients qcn environments

Force libcfs to create 2 CPU partitions
Setup mgs, mdt, osts
Start mds1: mount -t lustre -v lustre-wbcfs /mnt/lustre-mds1

OSD_WBC_PRIMARY_MDT=1
OSD_WBC_FSNAME=lustre
OSD_WBC_INDEX=0
svname -> lustre:MDT0000
OSD_WBC_MGS_NID=192.168.23.134@tcp
params -> mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp
arg[0] = /sbin/mount.lustre
arg[1] = -v
arg[2] = -o
arg[3] = rw
arg[4] = lustre-wbcfs
arg[5] = /mnt/lustre-mds1
source = lustre-wbcfs (lustre-wbcfs), target = /mnt/lustre-mds1
options(2/4096) = rw
checking for existing Lustre data: found
mounting device lustre-wbcfs at /mnt/lustre-mds1, flags=0x1000000 options=osd=osd-wbcfs,mgs,virgin,update,param=mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp,svname=lustre-MDT0000,device=lustre-wbcfs
Started wbcfs-target
Start ost1: mount -t lustre -v lustre-wbcfs /mnt/lustre-ost1
OSD_WBC_TGT_TYPE=OST
OSD_WBC_FSNAME=lustre
OSD_WBC_INDEX=0

OSD_WBC_MGS_NID=192.168.23.134@tcp

arg[0] = /sbin/mount.lustre
arg[1] = -v
arg[2] = -o
arg[3] = rw
arg[4] = lustre-wbcfs
arg[5] = /mnt/lustre-ost1
source = lustre-wbcfs (lustre-wbcfs), target = /mnt/lustre-ost1
options(2/4096) = rw
checking for existing Lustre data: found
mounting device lustre-wbcfs at /mnt/lustre-ost1, flags=0x1000000 options=osd=osd-wbcfs,mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp,virgin,update,param=mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp,svname=lustre-OST0000,device=lustre-wbcfs
Started wbcfs-target
Start ost2: mount -t lustre -v lustre-wbcfs /mnt/lustre-ost2
OSD_WBC_TGT_TYPE=OST
OSD_WBC_FSNAME=lustre
OSD_WBC_INDEX=1
svname -> lustre:OST0001
OSD_WBC_MGS_NID=192.168.23.134@tcp
params -> mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp
arg[0] = /sbin/mount.lustre
arg[1] = -v
arg[2] = -o
arg[3] = rw
arg[4] = lustre-wbcfs
arg[5] = /mnt/lustre-ost2
source = lustre-wbcfs (lustre-wbcfs), target = /mnt/lustre-ost2
options(2/4096) = rw
checking for existing Lustre data: found
mounting device lustre-wbcfs at /mnt/lustre-ost2, flags=0x1000000 options=osd=osd-wbcfs,mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp,virgin,update,param=mgsnode=192.168.23.134@tcp,svname=lustre-OST0001,device=lustre-wbcfs
Started wbcfs-target
Starting client: qcn:  -o user_xattr,flock 192.168.23.134@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
Using TIMEOUT=100
osc.lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff0000cca10000.idle_timeout=debug
osc.lustre-OST0001-osc-ffff0000cca10000.idle_timeout=debug
setting jobstats to procname_uid
Setting lustre.sys.jobid_var from disable to procname_uid
Waiting 90s for 'procname_uid'
Updated after 3s: want 'procname_uid' got 'procname_uid'
disable quota as required

# df
lustre-wbcfs                 3815536         0   3815536   0% /mnt/lustre-mds1
lustre-wbcfs                 3815536         0   3815536   0% /mnt/lustre-ost1
lustre-wbcfs                 3815536         0   3815536   0% /mnt/lustre-ost2
192.168.23.134@tcp:/lustre   7631072         0   7631072   0% /mnt/lustre

But there are some memory leak problem during cleanup. I will investigate later.

 

Regards,

Yingjin

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58439
Subject: LU-18813 osd-wbcfs: MemFS-based OSD with writeback support
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 57d8f3c2b7d26aef8da046480d3c5de9484fe553
If you have a prototype, I'd be interested to see it. Sharing code with MetaWBC is an interesting idea. How much do you have prototyped? Just bulk file data? Or indices and dirents as well?

I just did some prototype codes based on your in-mem OSD work.

and also it borrows some codes form client side MetaWBC work for the MemFS implementation.

Same as your work in-mem OSD, memory FS-based OSD can also build with client side code  of Lustre in the patchless manner.

MemFS-based OSD reused VFS (it is a little heavy?); while in-mem OSD is purely using data structures in kernel without any concept of FS.

I think both designs are worthy tying.

Is this a suggested design? Or something you already implemented? Using ramfs would allow us to reuse a lot of Linux VFS stuff. But I think there would be less opportunities to reuse code inside of Lustre.

In LU-17995, the in-mem OSD does not use any FS concept, the files and data are managed by some in-memory data structure such as list, rhashtable, and page array (for data), etc, al.

 

However, the design of memory FS based OSD is borrowed lots of idea from memory-based file systems such as ramfs/tmpfs. And lots of VFS code in Linux kernel can be reused in this design, such as:

  • Page caching management;
  • dcache
  • icache
  • Linux kernel write back codes
Isn't this a duplicate of LU-17995?
